utility_evaluation.REASONING:
Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 corresponding top-level items. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details:
- health_coverage: Requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. Plan specifies physical_health plan with price (€200/month), mental_health plan with price (€130/month), and total_health_cost (€330/month). Fully specified.
- property_coverage: Requires home/contents insurance with costs. Plan provides coverage description and a cost (€95/month). Fully specified.
- travel_coverage: Requires travel insurance with costs. Plan provides coverage description and cost (€115/month). Fully specified.
- additional_services: Requires any enhancement services with costs. Plan lists children_coverage details and the cost (€250/month). Fully specified.
- total_budget: Requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. Plan gives monthly and annual costs, plus previous cost and savings. Fully specified.
All 5/5 items are present and complete.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: We need to match each item named in the final coverage plan to the exact corresponding item name in the ground-truth ratings and return its numeric rating. The final plan includes: PrimeCare Premium Plan (physical health), PrimeCare Premium Mental Health (which corresponds to PrimeCare Premium Plan under mental_health), EliteHome Premium Coverage (property), PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel), and for children: LifeFirst Standard + CareCo Basic for Lucas and Isabella. Ground-truth ratings exist for: PrimeCare Premium Plan (physical health rating 10), PrimeCare Premium Plan under mental_health (rating 10), EliteHome Premium Coverage (rating 10), PrimeTravel Premium Plan (rating 10), LifeFirst Standard Plan (physical health rating 8) and CareCo Basic Plan (physical health rating 3). There is no separate combined product rating for “LifeFirst Standard + CareCo Basic”; we map each constituent plan to its rating. The plan lists two children each with the same combo, but ratings map to items, not per-person instances, so we include each unique plan once.
